summary: deployment-key-auditor exfiltrates host info and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: package.json line 31 contains an inline Node script (invoked via a lifecycle hook) that requires child_process, os, and https, collects hostname / id / process.cwd() / process.env, and POSTs the result to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js extends this behavior: it requires fs, os, https, and child_process; calls execSync (including `whoami`) and os.hostname() to fingerprint the host; uses fs.existsSync to probe filesystem locations; and issues https.request / https.get to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint (lines 142, 143, 161, 320). The destination is a GitHub Pages user site unrelated to any documented publisher and is functioning as a hardcoded C2/exfiltration endpoint. The combination of installer-environment scraping (process.env, hostname, whoami, cwd) plus a hardcoded non-publisher exfiltration URL reachable at install/lifecycle time is a credential/host-intel stealer. The package's stated purpose ("deployment key auditor") is the cover story.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
